article_title: GitHub Desktop 3.6 Adds Git Worktree Support and Moves Copilot Into Commit Authoring and Merge-Conflict Resolution

content_preview.title: GitHub Desktop 3.6 Adds Git Worktree Support and Moves Copilot Into Commit Authoring and Merge-Conflict Resolution
content_preview.summary: GitHub Desktop 3.6.0, released June 26, brings a graphical worktree workflow and routes Copilot into commit messages and merge conflicts, with a model picker and BYOK.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

GitHub has released version 3.6 of GitHub Desktop, its free graphical Git client, adding native Git worktree support and pushing GitHub Copilot deeper into everyday version-control tasks. According to the [GitHub Changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2026-06-26-github-desktop-3-6-worktrees-and-deeper-copilot-integration/), the update "brings more of your day-to-day Git flow into one place with GitHub Copilot now powering commit authoring and merge conflict resolution, plus new Gi...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) returned dead link errors — verify URLs before resubmitting
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece in the standard Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know format. Appropriate technical depth on worktrees and the Copilot SDK integration; 498 words, well within the News 400-1200 range. Every substantive claim is attributed inline to the cited source.
editor_notes.source_verification: Both automated snapshots FAILED at chief:review time (manifest.json records file: null for both sources — github.blog returned 'fetch failed' and windowsforum.com returned HTTP 403 bot-block). No .html.gz snapshots exist to read. As permitted for failed snapshots, I fell back to live WebFetch to verify. source-0 (github.blog changelog, the primary source): live WebFetch CONFIRMED all nine attributed claims/quotes verbatim — the summary line, macOS/Windows availability, the worktree description, the 'coding agents ... isolated, parallel sessions' line, the copilot-instructions.md/AGENTS.md custom-instructions claim, the merge-conflict 'review, accept, or edit' language, the Copilot SDK 'shared foundation' claim, the model-picker + BYOK sentence, and the 'free to download and use' pricing line. source-1 (windowsforum.com, secondary corroboration): live WebFetch CONFIRMED the June 26, 2026 release date, the BYOK statement, and the caution that a clean AI-suggested merge 'can still be wrong.' Both sources support what the article attributes to them.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No hallucinations detected. Every direct quote appears verbatim in the primary changelog. The version number (3.6.0), platforms (macOS/Windows), date (June 26, 2026), and all feature specifics trace to the cited sources. The 'What We Don't Know' section correctly frames the limits of GitHub's disclosure without inventing facts.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no editorializing. Properly hedges AI merge-resolution with the source's own caveat.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Not a duplicate. The May 16, 2026 article covers the SEPARATE standalone Copilot Desktop app (technical preview), which this article explicitly distinguishes from GitHub Desktop 3.6; the June 30 git-2.55 article is about Git core, not the GUI client. No overlap in event, sources, or angle.
concerns: windowsforum.com is not on config/source_allowlist.txt. Judged editorially acceptable: it is used only as secondary corroboration (date, BYOK, review caveat); the entire factual load of the article rests on the primary github.blog changelog, which is authoritative first-party sourcing. Allowlist NOT modified.

Both source snapshots failed to archive automatically (fetch failed / HTTP 403), so no local HTML snapshot is committed to the provenance chain for this article. Verification was performed via live WebFetch instead; the live URLs are valid, not dead.
recommendations: A corrections/clarification record is filed on merge to disclose that snapshots failed and verification was done against the live sources, and that a secondary source is off-allowlist — preserving a transparent provenance trail. No factual correction is warranted.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Content is factually clean and every claim is verbatim-verified against its cited source; the only issues are provenance-mechanical (failed auto-snapshots, one off-allowlist secondary source), not editorial. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS with a clarification note documenting the source-verification circumstances.
